pclmbayes 0.1.0

    • Bayesian density estimation and exact ungrouping with pclmbayes

Lead concentration in the blood of New York children, 1974

Source: R/data.R
bloodlead.Rd

Interval-censored measurements of lead concentration (in \(\mu\)g/dl) in the blood of young Puerto Ricans aged 1-12 years living in New York in 1974, as reported by Hasselblad et al. (1980) and re-used as Example 6.1 by Lambert and Eilers (2009).

Usage

bloodlead

Format

A data frame with 7 rows and 3 columns:

lower

Lower limit of the wide bin (\(\mu\)g/dl).

upper

Upper limit of the wide bin (\(\mu\)g/dl).

count

Observed frequency \(m_j\) in the bin.

Details

The original instrument's resolution was limited; observations were recorded only as falling into one of seven wide intervals. The last interval, 65+, was bounded above by 80 in the analysis of Lambert and Eilers (2009). Total \(n = 139\).

References

Hasselblad, V., Stead, A. G. and Galke, W. (1980). Analysis of coarsely grouped data from the lognormal distribution. Journal of the American Statistical Association, 75, 771–778.

Lambert, P. and Eilers, P. H. C. (2009). Bayesian density estimation from grouped continuous data. Computational Statistics and Data Analysis, 53(4), 1388–1399.

Examples

data(bloodlead)
bloodlead
#>   lower upper count
#> 1     0    15    27
#> 2    15    25    71
#> 3    25    35    32
#> 4    35    45     6
#> 5    45    55     3
#> 6    55    65     0
#> 7    65    80     0
